---
title: "Liu (2009) Simulation Study"
author: "Shozen Dan"
date: "2025-11-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(cmdstanr)
library(bayesplot)

REPO_DIR <- "~/Imperial/0_Research/modular_bayes/"
```

```{r experiment-config}
set.seed(1234)

config <- list(
    n1 = 25,
    n2 = 50,
    mu = 0,
    theta = 1,
    s1 = 1,
    s2 = 1
)
```

```{r}
# Function to generate data for the simulation study
generate_data <- function(n1, n2, mu, theta, s1, s2) {
    y1 <- rnorm(n1, mean = mu, sd = s1)
    y2 <- rnorm(n2, mean = mu + theta, sd = s2)

    return(list(y1 = y1, y2 = y2))
}

set.seed(1234)
data <- generate_data(
    n1 = config$n1,
    n2 = config$n2,
    mu = config$mu,
    theta = config$theta,
    s1 = config$s1,
    s2 = config$s2
)
```

```{r sanity-check}
ggplot() +
    geom_histogram(aes(x = data$y1), bins = 15, fill = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Histograms of Simulated Data", x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
    theme_bw()
```

```{r prepare-data-for-stan}
stan_data <- list(
    n1 = config$n1,
    y1 = data$y1
)
```

```{r fit-model}
# Full posterior
model_full <- cmdstan_model("../../stan/liu_m1.stan")
fit_full <- model_full$sample(
    data = stan_data,
    chains = 4,
    parallel_chains = 4,
    iter_warmup = 1000,
    iter_sampling = 1000,
    seed = 1234,
    show_messages=FALSE
)

model_gibbs <- cmdstan_model("../../stan/liu_m1_gibbs.stan")
fit_gibbs <- model_gibbs$sample(
    data = stan_data,
    chains = 4,
    parallel_chains = 4,
    iter_warmup = 1000,
    iter_sampling = 1000,
    seed = 1234,
    show_messages=FALSE
)
```

```{r posterior-summary}
# Full posterior
post_full <- fit_full$draws(format = "draws_matrix")
sum_full <- fit_full$summary("mu")
sum_full$post_type <- "full"

post_gibbs <- fit_gibbs$draws(format = "draws_matrix")
sum_gibbs <- fit_gibbs$summary("mu")
sum_gibbs$post_type <- "gibbs"

rbind(sum_full, sum_gibbs)
```

```{r posterior-plots}
df_post_full <- data.frame(
  post_type = "full",
  mu = as.numeric(post_full[,"mu"])
)

df_post_gibbs <- data.frame(
  post_type = "gibbs",
  mu = as.numeric(post_gibbs)
)

df_post <- rbind(df_post_full, df_post_gibbs)
```

```{r}
ggplot(df_post, aes(mu, fill = post_type, color = post_type)) +
  geom_density(alpha=0.7)
```
